skill.name: swiftui-expert-skill
skill.description: Guides SwiftUI implementation and review using modern state management, view composition, performance, navigation, and iOS 26 Liquid Glass practices.
skill.summary: Build and review SwiftUI code with modern Apple API guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown code formatting, Swift identifiers, and checklist text in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, command execution, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious instructions were found. The only URL is source metadata, so the skill is safe to publish with low residual risk.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to inline Markdown code spans and Swift examples, not Ruby shell backtick execution. The file is a guidance document and does not define executable scripts or command invocation logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reported locations are Markdown checklist items, inline code terms, and Swift snippets. No shell, Ruby, subprocess, or command execution mechanism is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts appear to match ordinary text and Markdown content, such as front matter and SwiftUI API names. No cryptographic implementation, hashing call, or insecure algorithm usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Semantic review found no crypto code or algorithm selection. The matches are documentation text, so the static category is not applicable.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Keywords in SwiftUI Guidance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts map to SwiftUI review guidance and API names, including screen sizing and debugging recommendations. They do not collect host data, enumerate networks, or inspect the runtime environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is static SwiftUI best-practice prose. There is no code path that performs system or network reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Low Risk: External Source URL Metadata
low_findings.description: The only hardcoded URL is the source repository reference in front matter. It documents provenance and is not used by code to make a network request.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears in YAML metadata as a repository source. No fetch, curl, download, or network execution behavior is present.

content.user_title: Improve SwiftUI Code with Modern Patterns
content.value_statement: SwiftUI projects can accumulate outdated APIs, fragile state, and slow view updates. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to review or implement SwiftUI using modern Apple patterns.
actual_capabilities: Reviews SwiftUI state management and property wrapper choices.
Recommends modern replacements for deprecated SwiftUI APIs.
Checks view composition, list identity, and layout patterns.
Identifies performance issues in SwiftUI view update paths.
Guides iOS 26 Liquid Glass adoption with availability fallbacks.
Provides focused review checklists for SwiftUI code quality.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not compile or run Swift projects.
It depends on the model receiving enough project context.
It does not replace Apple documentation for final API verification.
It avoids enforcing one application architecture.

use_cases.title: Review a SwiftUI Feature
use_cases.description: Find outdated APIs, risky state ownership, layout issues, and performance problems before merging.

use_cases.title: Modernize Existing Views
use_cases.description: Replace legacy SwiftUI patterns with modern APIs while preserving behavior and compatibility.

use_cases.title: Plan Liquid Glass Adoption
use_cases.description: Add iOS 26 visual effects only where appropriate, with availability checks and fallbacks.

prompt_templates.title: Basic SwiftUI Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single view
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this SwiftUI view for state management, modern API usage, accessibility, and simple performance issues. Explain the highest priority fixes first.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor an Existing View
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this SwiftUI code to use modern APIs, smaller subviews, stable list identity, and clearer data flow. Keep behavior unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Design a New Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a SwiftUI implementation plan for this feature. Include state ownership, view boundaries, async loading, navigation, and testable business logic.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Performance and Compatibility
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this SwiftUI flow for update fan-out, body work, list performance, geometry usage, iOS availability, and Liquid Glass fallback behavior.

output_examples.input: Review a SwiftUI list screen that filters items inside the view body.
output_examples.output: The review highlights unstable list work, recommends prefiltering data, confirms stable identifiers, and suggests smaller row views.

output_examples.input: Modernize a view using NavigationView, foregroundColor, and ObservableObject.
output_examples.output: The response proposes NavigationStack, foregroundStyle, and Observable-based state where deployment targets allow it.

output_examples.input: Add Liquid Glass styling to a toolbar for iOS 26 and older systems.
output_examples.output: The response describes availability checks, native glass effects for iOS 26, and material fallbacks for earlier versions.

best_practices: Provide deployment targets and relevant SwiftUI code before asking for modernization advice.
Ask for prioritized findings when reviewing large views or feature flows.
Request compatibility notes when using new SwiftUI or iOS 26 APIs.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to validate APIs without providing platform and Xcode context.
Do not use it as the only source for production release approval.
Do not apply Liquid Glass styling without a product requirement and fallback plan.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps build, refactor, and review SwiftUI code using modern Apple UI and state patterns.

faq.question: Can it run my iOS app?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and review comments, but it does not compile or execute projects.

faq.question: Does it require one architecture?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on state, composition, performance, and testability without mandating MVVM or another architecture.

faq.question: Can it help with older iOS targets?
faq.answer: Yes. Ask for deployment-aware advice, and it can suggest fallbacks for newer APIs.

faq.question: Does it support Liquid Glass?
faq.answer: Yes. It gives iOS 26 Liquid Glass guidance when the user explicitly requests that styling.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
